query: How frequently in 2026 has Russia launched overnight drone barrages against Ukraine exceeding 600 drones in a single night? List specific dates and drone counts for the largest barrages in May, June, and early July 2026 according to Ukrainian Air Force figures.
answer:
I've completed the research. Here is the summary of findings:

## Summary

**Direct answer to the question:** In 2026 (May–early July), confirmed single-night Russian drone barrages on Ukraine **exceeding ~600 drones** (per Ukrainian Air Force figures) were **rare and concentrated in mid-to-late May 2026** — only about **two nights** clearly reached/exceeded the 600 threshold.

### Largest single-night barrages by month (Ukrainian Air Force figures):

**May 2026 (escalation peak):**
- **May 15–16: 656 drones + 73 missiles** ✅ (>600)
- **May 23–24: 600 drones + 90 missiles** ✅ (=600, 690 total assets)
- May 13–14: 1,567 drones + 56 missiles — but this was a **cumulative ~24–30 hour total**, not a single overnight barrage

**June 2026:**
- Large-scale Russian strikes **notably lessened** (per ISW/Critical Threats). No confirmed single-night Russian barrage over 600 drones on Ukraine was found.

**Early July 2026 (resumption, but below 600/night):**
- July 1–2: **496 drones** + 74 missiles (killed 27+ in Kyiv)
- July 4–6: **351 drones** + 68 missiles (killed 22+; ballistic-missile focus)

### ⚠️ Two important cautions I flagged:
1. **The famous "728" and "741" drone records targeting Lutsk are from July 2025, not 2026** — they appear in searches due to date confusion.
2. **Many "660/556/430+ drone" figures from June–July 2026 are Ukrainian attacks ON Russia** (Russian MoD interception counts), not Russian barrages on Ukraine. Similarly, the 1,560–1,600 figures were multi-day cumulative totals, not single nights.

The full structured payload (key facts, sources, data points) has been submitted.

sources.title: Russia Launches 90 Missiles and 600 Drones Against Ukraine in Overnight Attack Targeting Kyiv (May 23–24, 2026)
sources.snippet: Russia launched a total of 690 aerial assets, including 90 missiles and 600 drones, according to the Ukrainian Air Force, on the night of May 23–24, 2026.

sources.title: Russia Launches 90 Missiles and 600 Drones at Ukraine Overnight, May 23–24, 2026
sources.snippet: According to the Ukrainian Air Force, Russia attacked Ukraine with 690 air attack assets, including 90 missiles and 600 drones.

sources.title: Ukraine's air force said Russia launched 656 drones and 73 missiles overnight (May 15–16, 2026)
sources.snippet: Ukraine's air force said Russia launched 656 drones and 73 missiles overnight.

sources.title: 14 May 2026 Russian strikes on Ukraine
sources.snippet: During the night of 13–14 May 2026, Russia conducted its 'largest aerial attack' with 1,567 drones and 56 missiles per Zelenskyy (cumulative).

sources.title: Russia hammers Ukraine in biggest prolonged drone attack since war began
sources.snippet: Zelensky said Russia fired more than 1,560 drones since the start of Wednesday — the largest aerial attack in a two-day period since the war began.

sources.title: Thursday, July 2. Russia's War On Ukraine
sources.snippet: Overnight on July 2, Russia attacked Ukraine with 74 missiles and 496 drones in the first sweeping attack in more than two weeks, according to Ukraine's Air Force.

sources.title: Russia's missile and drone attacks on Ukraine kill at least 22
sources.snippet: According to Ukraine's air force, Russia launched 351 drones and 68 missiles overnight targeting mainly Kyiv; all 29 ballistic missiles struck targets.

sources.title: ISW Russian Offensive Campaign Assessment, July 2, 2026
sources.snippet: The frequency of Russian large-scale drone and missile strikes against Ukraine lessened in June 2026 for unclear reasons.

sources.title: Ukraine's Air Force reports nearly 300 Russian drones used in overnight attack (May 15–16)
sources.snippet: Russian forces attacked Ukraine with 294 drones on the night of 15-16 May; 269 destroyed or jammed.

sources.title: Russia launches another record drone attack (728 drones, Lutsk) — dated July 9, 2025
sources.snippet: Russia fired a record 728 Shahed and decoy drones at Ukraine overnight (July 9, 2025), the Ukrainian air force said.

sources.title: Russian Army Downs Nearly 700 Ukrainian Drones Overnight (June 26, 2026)
sources.snippet: Russia's Defense Ministry said it downed 660 Ukrainian drones — a Ukrainian attack ON Russia, not Russian-on-Ukraine.

key_facts: Night of May 15–16, 2026: Russia launched 656 drones and 73 missiles at Ukraine per Ukraine's Air Force — a confirmed single-night barrage exceeding 600 drones.
Night of May 23–24, 2026: Russia launched 600 drones and 90 missiles (690 total aerial assets) at Ukraine per the Ukrainian Air Force, primarily targeting Kyiv — at the 600 threshold.
May 13–14, 2026: The '1,567 drones + 56 missiles' figure cited by Zelenskyy as the 'largest aerial attack of the war' was a cumulative ~24–30 hour total, not a single overnight barrage.

June 2026: ISW/Critical Threats assessed that the frequency of Russian large-scale drone and missile strikes on Ukraine LESSENED for unclear reasons; no confirmed single-night Russian barrage over 600 drones on Ukraine was found for June 2026.
July 1–2, 2026: Russia launched 496 drones and 74 missiles at Ukraine per Ukraine's Air Force — the first sweeping attack in more than two weeks; killed 27+ in Kyiv. Below 600 drones.
July 4–6, 2026: Russia launched 351 drones and 68 missiles at Ukraine per Ukraine's Air Force; killed 22+; emphasis on ballistic missiles. Below 600 drones.
The widely cited 728-drone (July 9) and 741-aerial-weapon record barrages on Lutsk are from JULY 2025, not 2026, and appear in searches due to date confusion.
The 660-drone, 556-drone, and 430+-drone figures from June–July 2026 refer to UKRAINIAN attacks on Russia (Russian MoD interception counts), not Russian barrages on Ukraine.
The night of May 15–16 also had a smaller ~294-drone night reported separately, showing wide night-to-night variation during the May escalation.
